curriculum.subject: Biology
curriculum.overallDescription: 9 units, 23 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.

units.description: The Living World, Biological Classification, Plant Kingdom, Animal Kingdom

units.unitName: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals
units.description: Morphology of Flowering Plants, Anatomy of Flowering Plants, Structural Organisation in Animals
Cell-The Unit of Life, Biomolecules, Cell Cycle and Cell Division
Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Respiration in Plants, Plant - Growth and Development
Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Evolution
Human Health and Diseases, Microbes in Human Welfare
Biotechnology - Principles and Processes, Biotechnology and its Applications
Organisms and Populations, Ecosystem, Biodiversity and its Conservation

Three domains of life, taxonomy and systematics
Concept of species and taxonomical hierarchy
Binomial nomenclature

Classification into major groups with examples
Lichens, Viruses and Viroids
Classification of plants into major groups

Classification of chordates upto class level

units.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals

key_concepts: Morphology of root, stem and leaf
Inflorescence types
Structure of flower, fruit and seed
Description of family Solanaceae
Tissue systems in dicots and monocots
Structure and functions of tissue systems
Morphology and Anatomy of frog systems
Functions of digestive, circulatory, respiratory, nervous and reproductive systems
Cell theory and cell as basic unit of life
Structure of prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells (Plant vs Animal)
Cell envelope, membrane and wall structure
Organelles: Endomembrane system, ER, Golgi, Lysosomes, Vacuoles, Mitochondria, Ribosomes, Plastids, Microbodies
Chemical constituents: Proteins, Carbohydrates, Lipids, Nucleic acids (structure and function)
Enzyme types, properties and action
Phases of cell cycle
Mitosis and its significance
Meiosis and its significance
Autotrophic nutrition and site of photosynthesis
Pigments involved (elementary idea)
Photochemical and biosynthetic phases, cyclic/non-cyclic photophosphorylation
Chemiosmotic hypothesis, Photorespiration, C3 and C4 pathways
Exchange of gases and cellular respiration (Glycolysis, Fermentation)
TCA cycle and Electron Transport System
Energy relations: ATP generation count
Amphibolic pathways and Respiratory Quotient
Seed germination, phases of growth and growth rate
Conditions for differentiation, dedifferentiation and redifferentiation
Plant growth regulators: Auxin, Gibberellin, Cytokinin, Ethylene, ABA

Mendelian inheritance, deviations from Mendelism (incomplete dominance, co-dominance), multiple alleles and blood groups, pleiotropy, polygenic inheritance, chromosome theory of inheritance, sex determination mechanisms
Linkage and crossing over, sex-linked inheritance (haemophilia, colour blindness)
Mendelian disorders in humans (thalassemia), chromosomal disorders (Down's syndrome, Turner's, Klinefelter's syndromes)
Search for genetic material, DNA as genetic material; Structure and packaging of DNA/RNA
DNA replication, Central Dogma (transcription, translation), Genetic code
Gene expression and regulation (lac operon)
Genome projects (Human and rice genome) and DNA fingerprinting
Origin of life, evidences for biological evolution (paleontology, comparative anatomy, embryology, molecular)
Darwin's contribution and modern synthetic theory of evolution
Mechanism of evolution: variation through mutation and recombination
Digestion and absorption processes (alimentary canal, enzymes, hormones)
Nutritional disorders: PEM, indigestion, constipation, vomiting, jaundice, diarrhea
Role of microbes in human welfare

practicals.title: Study and describe locally available common flowering plants
practicals.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals
practicals.details: Dissection and display of floral whorls, anther and ovary to show number of chambers (floral formulae and floral diagrams), type of root, stem, leaf arrangement.

practicals.title: Preparation and study of T.S. of dicot and monocot roots and stems
practicals.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals
practicals.details: Primary structure preparation.

practicals.title: Study of osmosis by potato osmometer
practicals.details: Experiment on endosmosis in raisins or potato osmometer.

practicals.title: Study of plasmolysis in epidermal peels
practicals.details: Using Rhoeo/lily leaves or flashy scale leaves of onion bulb.

practicals.title: Study of distribution of stomata on the upper and lower surfaces of leaves
Comparative study of the rates of transpiration in the upper and lower surfaces of leaves
Test for the presence of sugar, starch, proteins and fats
practicals.details: In suitable plant and animal materials.

practicals.title: Separation of plant pigments through paper chromatography
Study of the rate of respiration in flower buds/leaf tissue and germinating seeds
Test for presence of urea, sugar, albumin, bile salts in urine
practicals.details: Tests on human excretory products.

practicals.title: Parts of a compound microscope
practicals.details: Identification and familiarity with apparatus (Spotting).

practicals.title: Specimens/slides/models identification: Bacteria to Rabbit
practicals.details: Bacteria, Oscillatoria, Spirogyra, Rhizopus, mushroom, yeast, liverwort, moss, fern, pine, monocot/dicot plants, lichen; Virtual specimens: Amoeba, Hydra, Ascaris, earthworm, prawn, silkworm, honey bee, snail, starfish, shark, rohu, frog, lizard, pigeon, rabbit.

practicals.title: Mitosis in onion root tip cells and animal's cells
practicals.details: From permanent slides (grasshopper).

practicals.title: Types of inflorescence (cymose and racemose)
practicals.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals

practicals.title: Human skeleton and different types of joints
practicals.details: Using virtual images/models only.

practicals.title: Study locally available common flowering plants of the family Solanaceae
practicals.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals
practicals.details: Identify type of stem (Herbaceous or Woody), type of leaves (Compound or Simple).

practicals.title: Study the parts of a compound microscope
practicals.details: Eye piece and objective lens, mirror, stage, coarse and fine adjustment knobs.

practicals.title: Differentiate between monocot and dicot plants on the basis of venation patterns
Study parts of human skeleton (Model)
practicals.details: Ball and socket joints of thigh and shoulder; Rib cage.

practicals.title: Study honeybee/butterfly, snail/sheik snail through shell, Starfish, Pigeon
practicals.details: Through models (Visually Impaired Practicals).

practicals.title: Identify the given specimen of a fungus and gymnosperm
practicals.details: Mushroom, pine cone.

practicals.title: Relate experimental set up with aim: Potato Osmometer/endosmosis in raisins
Prepare a temporary mount to observe pollen germination
Study the plant population density by quadrat method
Study the plant population frequency by quadrat method
Prepare a temporary mount of onion root tip to study mitosis
Isolate DNA from available plant material
practicals.details: Spinach, green pea seeds, papaya, banana etc.

practicals.title: Flowers adapted to pollination by different agencies
practicals.details: Wind, insects, birds (Spotting).

practicals.title: Pollen germination on stigma through a permanent slide or scanning electron micrograph
Identification of stages of gamete development (T.S. of testis and ovary)
practicals.details: From grasshopper/mice permanent slides.

practicals.title: Meiosis in onion bud cell or grasshopper testis through permanent slides
T.S. of blastula through permanent slides (Mammalian)
Mendelian inheritance using seeds
practicals.details: Using seeds of different colour/sizes.

practicals.title: Prepared pedigree charts of genetic traits
practicals.details: Rolling of tongue, blood groups, ear lobes, widow's peak and colour blindness.

practicals.title: Controlled pollination - emasculation, tagging and bagging
Common disease causing organisms identification
practicals.details: Ascaris, Entamoeba, Plasmodium, fungus (ringworm) through permanent slides, models or virtual images.

practicals.title: Models specimens showing symbiotic association and parasitic mode
practicals.details: Lichens, root nodules of leguminous plants, Cuscuta on host.

practicals.title: Flash cards / models showing examples of homologous and analogous organs

projects.title: Investigatory Project
projects.details: Project Record + Viva Voce (Credit to the student's work over the academic session may be given)

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit I (Diversity of Living Organisms): 15 marks; Unit II (Structural Organization in Plants and Animals): 10 marks; Unit III (Cell: Structure and Function): 15 marks; Unit IV (Plant Physiology): 12 marks; Unit V (Human Physiology): 18 marks.

Class XI Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting Part B: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.

Class XII Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit VI (Reproduction): 16 marks; Unit VII (Genetics and Evolution): 20 marks; Unit VIII (Biology and Human Welfare): 12 marks; Unit IX (Biotechnology and its Applications): 12 marks; Unit X (Ecology and Environment): 10 marks.

Class XII Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.
question_paper_design: Class XI (2026-27): Typology includes VSA including MCQs, Assertion–Reasoning type questions, SA, LA-I, LA-II; Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based/Integrated assessment questions. Internal choice of approximately 33% provided.

Class XII (2026-27): Competency weightage: Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding - 50%; Application of Knowledge / Concepts - 30%; Analyse, Evaluate and Create - 20%. Typology includes VSA including MCQs, Assertion–Reasoning type questions, SA, LA-I, LA-II; Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based/Integrated assessment questions. Internal choice of approximately 33% provided.
competency_weightage: Class XII Theory: Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding (50%), Application of Knowledge / Concepts (30%), Analyse, Evaluate and Create (20%).

Suggestive verbs for Class XII: Knowledge/Understanding - State, name, list, identify, define, suggest, describe, outline, summarize; Application - Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain, distinguish; Analysis/Evaluation/Create - Interpret, analyse, compare, contrast, examine, evaluate, discuss, construct.
internal_assessment: Topics included in syllabus but assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments: Class XI - Digestion and Absorption; Class XII - Environmental Issues (Air pollution, Water pollution, Solid Wastes, Agro-chemicals, Radioactive wastes, Greenhouse effect, Ozone depletion, Degradation by improper resource utilization, deforestation).
Internal choice of approximately 33% provided in question papers for both classes.
practical_assessment: Class XI: One Major Experiment (5 marks), One Minor Experiment (4 marks), Slide Preparation (5 marks), Spotting Part B (7 marks).
Class XII: One Major Experiment (5 marks), One Minor Experiment (4 marks), Slide Preparation (5 marks), Spotting (7 marks).

Visually Impaired Students Class XI & XII: Time 2 Hours, Max Marks 30. Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (5 marks), Written test based on prescribed practicals (10 marks), Practical Records (5 marks), Viva (10 marks).
project_assessment: Class XI Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.
Class XII Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.

Class XII Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.

Class XII (2026-27): Competency weightage: Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding - 50%; Application of Knowledge / Concepts - 30%; Analyse, Evaluate and Create - 20%. Typology includes VSA including MCQs, Assertion–Reasoning type questions, SA, LA-I, LA-II; Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based/Integrated assessment questions. Internal choice of approximately 33% provided.
competency_weightage: Class XII Theory: Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding (50%), Application of Knowledge / Concepts (30%), Analyse, Evaluate and Create (20%).

Suggestive verbs for Class XII: Knowledge/Understanding - State, name, list, identify, define, suggest, describe, outline, summarize; Application - Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain, distinguish; Analysis/Evaluation/Create - Interpret, analyse, compare, contrast, examine, evaluate, discuss, construct.
internal_assessment: Topics included in syllabus but assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments: Class XI - Digestion and Absorption; Class XII - Environmental Issues (Air pollution, Water pollution, Solid Wastes, Agro-chemicals, Radioactive wastes, Greenhouse effect, Ozone depletion, Degradation by improper resource utilization, deforestation).
Internal choice of approximately 33% provided in question papers for both classes.
practical_assessment: Class XI: One Major Experiment (5 marks), One Minor Experiment (4 marks), Slide Preparation (5 marks), Spotting Part B (7 marks).
Class XII: One Major Experiment (5 marks), One Minor Experiment (4 marks), Slide Preparation (5 marks), Spotting (7 marks).

Visually Impaired Students Class XI & XII: Time 2 Hours, Max Marks 30. Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (5 marks), Written test based on prescribed practicals (10 marks), Practical Records (5 marks), Viva (10 marks).
project_assessment: Class XI Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.
Class XII Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.

competency_groups.title: Explores the structure and function of the living world at the cellular level
competency_groups.description: C-3.1 Explains the role of cellular components (nucleus, mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, vacuoles, chloroplast, cell wall), including the semi permeability of cell membrane in making cell the structural basis of living organisms and functional basis of life processes
competencies: C-3.1 Explains the role of cellular components (nucleus, mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, vacuoles, chloroplast, cell wall), including the semi permeability of cell membrane in making cell the structural basis of living organisms and functional basis of life processes

C-3.2 Analyses similarities and differences in the life processes involved in nutrition (photosynthesis in plants; absorption of nutrients in fungi; digestion in animals), transport (transport of water in plants; circulation in animals), exchange of materials (respiration and excretion), and reproduction
C-3.3 Describes mechanisms of heredity (in terms of DNA, genes, chromosomes) and variation (as changes in the sequence of DNA)

competency_groups.title: Explores interconnectedness between organisms and their environment
competency_groups.description: C-4.1 Applies the knowledge of cellular diversity in organisms along with the ecological role organisms play (autotrophic/ heterotrophic nutrition) to classify them into five-kingdoms; C-4.2 Illustrates different levels of organisations of living organisms (from molecules to organisms); C-4.3 Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes along with interactions that take place at each level
competencies: C-4.1 Applies the knowledge of cellular diversity in organisms along with the ecological role organisms play (autotrophic/ heterotrophic nutrition) to classify them into five-kingdoms
C-4.2 Illustrates different levels of organisations of living organisms (from molecules to organisms)
C-4.3 Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes along with interactions that take place at each level

competency_groups.title: Draws linkages between scientific knowledge and knowledge across other curricular areas
competency_groups.description: C-5.3 Applies scientific principles to explain phenomena in other subjects (sound pitch, octave, and amplitude in music; use of muscles in dance form and sports)
competencies: C-5.3 Applies scientific principles to explain phenomena in other subjects (sound pitch, octave, and amplitude in music; use of muscles in dance form and sports)

competency_groups.title: Understands and appreciates the contribution of India through history and the present times to the overall field of Science, including the disciplines that constitute it
competency_groups.description: C-6.1 Knows and explains the significant contributions of India to all matters (concepts, explanations, methods) that are studied within the curriculum in an integrated manner
competencies: C-6.1 Knows and explains the significant contributions of India to all matters (concepts, explanations, methods) that are studied within the curriculum in an integrated manner

competency_groups.title: Develops awareness of the most current discoveries, ideas, and frontiers in all areas of scientific knowledge in order to appreciate that Science is ever evolving, and that there are still many unanswered questions
competency_groups.description: C-7.1 States concepts that represent the most current understanding of the matter being studied — ranging from mere familiarity to conceptual understanding of the matter as appropriate to the developmental stage of the students; C-7.2 States questions related to matters in the curriculum for which current scientific understanding is well-recognised to be inadequate
competencies: C-7.1 States concepts that represent the most current understanding of the matter being studied — ranging from mere familiarity to conceptual understanding of the matter as appropriate to the developmental stage of the students
C-7.2 States questions related to matters in the curriculum for which current scientific understanding is well-recognised to be inadequate

competency_groups.title: Explores the nature of Science by doing Science
competency_groups.description: C-8.1 Develops accurate and appropriate models (including geometric, mathematical, graphical) to represent real-life events and phenomena using scientific principles and use these models to manipulate variables and predict results; C-8.2 Designs and implements a plan for scientific inquiry (formulates hypotheses, makes predictions, identifies variables, accurately uses scientific instruments, represents data — primary and secondary — in multiple modes, draws inferences based on data and understanding of scientific concepts, theories, laws, and principles, communicates findings using scientific terminology)
competencies: C-8.1 Develops accurate and appropriate models (including geometric, mathematical, graphical) to represent real-life events and phenomena using scientific principles and use these models to manipulate variables and predict results

C-8.2 Designs and implements a plan for scientific inquiry (formulates hypotheses, makes predictions, identifies variables, accurately uses scientific instruments, represents data — primary and secondary — in multiple modes, draws inferences based on data and understanding of scientific concepts, theories, laws, and principles, communicates findings using scientific terminology)

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit I (Diversity of Living Organisms): 15 marks; Unit II (Structural Organization in Plants and Animals): 10 marks; Unit III (Cell: Structure and Function): 15 marks; Unit IV (Plant Physiology): 12 marks; Unit V (Human Physiology): 18 marks.

Class XI Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting Part B: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.

Class XII Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit VI (Reproduction): 16 marks; Unit VII (Genetics and Evolution): 20 marks; Unit VIII (Biology and Human Welfare): 12 marks; Unit IX (Biotechnology and its Applications): 12 marks; Unit X (Ecology and Environment): 10 marks.

projects.title: Investigatory Project
projects.details: Project Record + Viva Voce (Credit to the student's work over the academic session may be given)

practicals.title: Study and describe locally available common flowering plants
practicals.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals
practicals.details: Dissection and display of floral whorls, anther and ovary to show number of chambers (floral formulae and floral diagrams), type of root, stem, leaf arrangement.

practicals.title: Preparation and study of T.S. of dicot and monocot roots and stems
practicals.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals
practicals.details: Primary structure preparation.

practicals.title: Study of osmosis by potato osmometer
practicals.details: Experiment on endosmosis in raisins or potato osmometer.

practicals.title: Study of plasmolysis in epidermal peels
practicals.details: Using Rhoeo/lily leaves or flashy scale leaves of onion bulb.

practicals.title: Study of distribution of stomata on the upper and lower surfaces of leaves
Comparative study of the rates of transpiration in the upper and lower surfaces of leaves
Test for the presence of sugar, starch, proteins and fats
practicals.details: In suitable plant and animal materials.

practicals.title: Separation of plant pigments through paper chromatography
Study of the rate of respiration in flower buds/leaf tissue and germinating seeds
Test for presence of urea, sugar, albumin, bile salts in urine
practicals.details: Tests on human excretory products.

practicals.title: Parts of a compound microscope
practicals.details: Identification and familiarity with apparatus (Spotting).

practicals.title: Specimens/slides/models identification: Bacteria to Rabbit
practicals.details: Bacteria, Oscillatoria, Spirogyra, Rhizopus, mushroom, yeast, liverwort, moss, fern, pine, monocot/dicot plants, lichen; Virtual specimens: Amoeba, Hydra, Ascaris, earthworm, prawn, silkworm, honey bee, snail, starfish, shark, rohu, frog, lizard, pigeon, rabbit.

practicals.title: Mitosis in onion root tip cells and animal's cells
practicals.details: From permanent slides (grasshopper).

practicals.title: Types of inflorescence (cymose and racemose)
practicals.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals

practicals.title: Human skeleton and different types of joints
practicals.details: Using virtual images/models only.

practicals.title: Study locally available common flowering plants of the family Solanaceae
practicals.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals
practicals.details: Identify type of stem (Herbaceous or Woody), type of leaves (Compound or Simple).

practicals.title: Study the parts of a compound microscope
practicals.details: Eye piece and objective lens, mirror, stage, coarse and fine adjustment knobs.

practicals.title: Differentiate between monocot and dicot plants on the basis of venation patterns
Study parts of human skeleton (Model)
practicals.details: Ball and socket joints of thigh and shoulder; Rib cage.

practicals.title: Study honeybee/butterfly, snail/sheik snail through shell, Starfish, Pigeon
practicals.details: Through models (Visually Impaired Practicals).

practicals.title: Identify the given specimen of a fungus and gymnosperm
practicals.details: Mushroom, pine cone.

practicals.title: Relate experimental set up with aim: Potato Osmometer/endosmosis in raisins
Prepare a temporary mount to observe pollen germination
Study the plant population density by quadrat method
Study the plant population frequency by quadrat method
Prepare a temporary mount of onion root tip to study mitosis
Isolate DNA from available plant material
practicals.details: Spinach, green pea seeds, papaya, banana etc.

practicals.title: Flowers adapted to pollination by different agencies
practicals.details: Wind, insects, birds (Spotting).

practicals.title: Pollen germination on stigma through a permanent slide or scanning electron micrograph
Identification of stages of gamete development (T.S. of testis and ovary)
practicals.details: From grasshopper/mice permanent slides.

practicals.title: Meiosis in onion bud cell or grasshopper testis through permanent slides
T.S. of blastula through permanent slides (Mammalian)
Mendelian inheritance using seeds
practicals.details: Using seeds of different colour/sizes.

practicals.title: Prepared pedigree charts of genetic traits
practicals.details: Rolling of tongue, blood groups, ear lobes, widow's peak and colour blindness.

practicals.title: Controlled pollination - emasculation, tagging and bagging
Common disease causing organisms identification
practicals.details: Ascaris, Entamoeba, Plasmodium, fungus (ringworm) through permanent slides, models or virtual images.

practicals.title: Models specimens showing symbiotic association and parasitic mode
practicals.details: Lichens, root nodules of leguminous plants, Cuscuta on host.

practicals.title: Flash cards / models showing examples of homologous and analogous organs
